target.name: New Limit
target.founder: Brian Armstrong, Blake Byers, Jacob Kimmel

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $810M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
New Limit is a Series B biotech & health company currently valued at $810M post-money, having raised $170M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates against a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector. Private peers range from Profluent at $600M to Eikon Therapeutics at $3.3B, establishing a wide dispersion characteristic of early-stage biotech.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $810M last post-money valuation, implying $1.5B for the next funding stage. Peer-median anchoring across four same-stage, same-sector companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 6.6x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, standard for private biotech companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.8B, with a median of $1.4B. This represents a 37–122% step-up from current valuation. Key uncertainties include the extreme width of peer valuations (5.5x spread), reflecting stage-dependent risk and scientific progress variability in early biotech, and the lack of revenue visibility that would anchor public-market multiples.
